David Ohm, PhD 

☰
    • About
    • Blog
    • Papers

My interests span the column space of a rank N matrix between engineering and physics. I try to keep that matrix symmetric so that the sum of eigenvalues equal the trace - maintaining a good work-life balance.

I grew up when MTV played heavy metal music videos late at night. That likely has nothing to do with anything, but I did go on to graduate with a Bachelor’s degree in Physics with an emphasis in lasers and optics, then completed a Master’s and PhD in Electrical Engineering with an obsessive focus on statistical signal processing. Regardless of all my previous playtime with lasers, I can still see out of both my eyes and I am currently the co-founder of a technology company that develops innovative capabilities for extracting information from sensor data. How fun is that!

I hold firm to the rule that when software is involved, misery is conserved. However, the proof of this theorem is too messy to fit on a T-shirt.

I also enjoy creating music and occasionally spending time in the mountains and on the rivers.
